growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with pyruvate as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: Both Ancylomarina euxinus strain M2P and Labilibaculum euxinus strain SYP grew well on pyruvate (doubling time 1.5-2.0 h; Fig. S20 A, B) and relatively slower on glucose (doubling time, 1.8-2.0 h)
growth_records.notes: Pyruvate doubling time 1.5-2.0 h; midpoint 1.75 h; mu = 0.693/1.75 = 0.396 h-1. Could not grow on cellobiose/cellulose.

growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with glucose as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: grew well on pyruvate (doubling time 1.5-2.0 h) and relatively slower on glucose (doubling time, 1.8-2.0 h; Fig. S20 A, B), but could not grow on cellobiose
growth_records.notes: Glucose doubling time 1.8-2.0 h; midpoint 1.9 h; mu = 0.693/1.9 = 0.365 h-1 (range 0.347-0.385). Same growth-curve figure covers Labilibaculum euxinus strain SYP with identical reported ranges.

phenotypes.citation: BacDive strain 164306 (Reimer LC et al., BacDive 2022, NAR); https://bacdive.dsmz.de/strain/164306
